programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: WCW Main Event - 6x36 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x37 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x38 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x39 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x40 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x41 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x43 - WCW Main Event
Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
programme.desc: After a family tragedy, three generations of the Deetz family return home to Winter River. Still haunted by Beetlejuice, Lydia's life is turned upside down when her teenage daughter, Astrid, accidentally opens the portal to the Afterlife.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds
programme.desc: At Adams College, the jocks rule the school from their house on high, the Alpha Beta fraternity. So when a group of socially-challenged misfits try to go Greek, they're instantly rejected by every house on campus. Deciding to start their own fraternity to protect their outcast brothers, the campus nerds soon find themselves in a battle royale as the Alpha Betas try to crush their new rivals.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Home Alone
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Kevin McCallister makes the most of the situation after his family unwittingly leaves him behind when they go on Christmas vacation. When thieves try to break into his home, he puts up a fight like no other.

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
programme.desc: A brash, cocky high school senior, tired of skipping school to spend a boring day at home, is determined to enjoy an epic day roaring around his favorite Chicago sites, enlisting his best friend and girlfriend to join him on the adventure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Monty Python's Life of Brian
programme.desc: Brian Cohen is an average young Jewish man, but through a series of ridiculous events, he gains a reputation as the Messiah. When he's not dodging his followers or being scolded by his shrill mother, the hapless Brian has to contend with the pompous Pontius Pilate and acronym-obsessed members of a separatist movement. Rife with Monty Python's signature absurdity, the tale finds Brian's life paralleling Biblical lore, albeit with many more laughs.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: When the nefarious Dark Helmet hatches a plan to snatch Princess Vespa and steal her planet's air, space-bum-for-hire Lone Starr and his clueless sidekick fly to the rescue. Along the way, they meet Yogurt, who puts Lone Starr wise to the power of "The Schwartz." Can he master it in time to save the day?

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
Columbo
programme.desc: An art critic, Dale Kingston, kills his uncle for his valuable collection of paintings. All the clues lead to the dead man's first wife. Can Columbo correct the Lieutenant's error before it is too late?

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: For their 5th wedding anniversary, Maude makes a big mistake in giving Walter an electric organ as a gift. He's driving everyone up the wall with his constant playing. Maude soon confronts him and they end up a huge argument, leading to Walter storming out and crashing his car into a tree.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: An office party on Christmas Eve at the Findlays home sets the stage for Walter's employees to break the news that they are planning to be unionized. When Walter learns this he decides to fire all of them, however, there is yet another issue at hand, Maude is on there side.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Sophia got the girls a chance to appear on a hit game show Grab that Dough in California. At the hotel, they miss their reservations and they must sleep in the lobby...as a result, all their personal belongings are stolen. Sophia still has the tickets but once they arrive to the game, the girls fight and split into two separate competing teams.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Sophia's brother, Angelo, a priest, is coming to Miami for a visit and he doesn't know Dorothy and Stan are divorced. So in order to spare his feelings, Sophia convinces Dorothy and Stan to pretend to be married. Meanwhile, Rose and Blanche prepare to star in a play, The Sound of Music.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Rose organizes a romantic getaway night at the hotel with Dr. Ursula as a guest to give a lecture. Blanche is excited because Taylor Michaels is coming to town. She once lost this man to another woman but Rose is the one Taylor is interested in when he arrives.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home for the Aged
programme.desc: Lamont breaks the news to Fred that he plans to join the crew of a tramp steamer and sail around the world. A shocked Fred gets another shock, Lamont plans on putting him in a retirement home.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith's religious faith is shaken after her friend female impersonator Beverly LaSalle is brutally murdered by street thungs at Christmas.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike recovers from the injuries he sustained in the mugging. But the Christmas Day gift exchange is still strained due to Edith's depression due to Beverly Lasalle's death, so the family rallies around her.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith is chosen to appear in a tv commericial but finds herself unable to lie when she begins to doubt the quality of the sponsor's detergent.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Lisa & Mary vs. Lori & Ben vs. Richard & Mark
programme.desc: Richard & Mark would later appear in the Sweep Of Champions.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Sandy & Tina vs. Sherra & Janie vs. Liza & Michael

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are greeting card salesmen who are mistakenly inducted into the army after escaping from the jealous husband of one of their customers. In bootcamp their sergeant turns out to be the same man, whom they constantly vex and bewilder. When the boys are sent to the front lines and the sergeant is captured they must rescue him, which they do after doping themselves with laughing gas. At the end they get shot off into the sunset on a cannon shell. Greeting card salesmen Larry, Moe and Curly hide from an irate man in what they think is a bread line and find themselves inducted into military service. Title is a parody of MGM film “Babes in Arms” (1939) starring Judy Garland and Mickey Rooney.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are traveling salesmen stranded in Valeska, a tropical country prone to earthquakes. Having no luck selling fur coats to the natives they are arrested when they receive a telegram instructing them to "get rid of present wardrobe" and an official thinks they are planning to assassinate president Ward Robey. With the help of Rita, a beautiful revolutionary, the boys escape a firing squad, and are sent on a mission to deliver important plans to the revolutionary leader. When they deliver a rolled up calendar by mistake, they are once again heading for a firing squad but are spared when Rita arrives with the real plans.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooge's friend Claude sells them his old lemon of a car so they can take Shemp, who is sick with a toothache, camping. The car won't work and the boys are apparently out a bundle, when a car collector happens on the scene and offers to buy it at a premium. Claude backs out on the deal and gives the stooges their money back, only to discover the "collector" is an escaped lunatic.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Told in flash back, the stooges tell their son how he came to have three fathers. The stooges, owners of a pawn shop, owed money to the gashouse protection society, a bunch of loan sharks. To complicate matters, a lady leaves a baby in the shop as part of a plan to sell a phony diamond and the stooges wind up caring for the kid. The stooges manage to defeat the crooks and when they finish telling the story, the kid goes off to find his real mother.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are actors traveling to perform at a fiesta in Mexico. After they accidentally switch suitcases with that of Dolores, a lovely senorita they met on trip down, they must sneak into her house to retrieve their suitcase. When they are confronted by her jealous husband he vows to kill them if he sees them again. At the fiesta where they are performing a comedy bullfight (Curly is the matador, Moe and Larry are in a bull costume) the husband bribes the attendants to let a real bull into the ring. Curly knocks the bull out with a head butt and becomes a hero.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Shemp dies but cannot get into heaven until he reforms Moe and Larry. He returns to earth as an invisible spirit and sets out to prevent the other two stooges, who are in league with the devil, from selling a phony invention (a fountain pen that writes under whip cream) to a rich couple. Shemp sabotages Moe and Larry' plans and makes it through the pearly gates.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges decide to get some easy money by having Curly slip on a bar of soap in a hotel lobby so they can sue the owner. Curly slips as planned but the hotel turns out to be run by an old lady who is about to lose her lease to the evil landlord. The stooges decide to help her fix up the place and start by beating up the landlord and stealing his watch. After their usual antics in renovating the place, the hotel is ready for the grand re-opening. The stooges put on a big show with a famous critic in attendance. Their corny act goes over poorly until Curly accidentally puts on a magicians coat and becomes a sensation and the place is a success.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene does NOT serve meat at the counter. She is forced to work there by Roseanne, but out of principal, will not serve the meat. That was her whole point

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Wait Till Your Father Gets Home
programme.desc: The death of their father finds Jackie blubbering, but Roseanne angry -- and sharing a drink with the "other woman" in his life.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: A wealthy and successful cousin whom Roseanne hasn't seen in 25 years comes to bury the hachet, and encourages Darlene to leave home.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Conners and the Tildens travel to California in a motor home, with Dan pondering his future and Ty contemplating Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: David's parents are moving to Chicago, but David doesn't want to join them. Darlene asks if David can move in with the Conners, but Roseanne and Dan declare it out of the question. Roseanne later learns that David is planning to just run away, so she meets with his mother who comes off as a mean and abusive drunk. She treats David so poorly that Roseanne changes her mind and allows David to move into the Conner house.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: It was Twenty Years Ago Today
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan plan a romantic dinner date to celebrate their twentieth wedding anniversary, but struggle with what to give one another. Dan finds out that Roseanne has decided on something expensive for him, so in order to get Roseanne a nice gift too, he takes a temporary job at Maury's Big and Tall store. His gift idea (and the job to pay for it), which seemed like a good idea at the time, soon blows up in his face.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene wants nothing to do with David after Molly kisses him; and a new friend puts a smile on Bev's face.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene springs a prom-night surprise on David: she has rented a motel room. Meanwhile, Dan is reluctant to share with Roseanne his plans for a new business.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: With the high transport fees, the staff have taken drastic measures to get to and from work without going broke. To help with cutting the expense, Mr.Grace has offered small apartments for the staff. With only the basic residence fees, the staff are quite excited about living together. But they'll soon find out that being neighbors has its ups, and downs.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr. Spooner faces the prospect of being sacked, but after a random musical performance he might just have a chance at stardom. With some help from the staff in this final episode, will he be rescued from misfortune?

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill and Toony show six cartoons featuring female characters.
Featured cartoons:
Muzzle Tough (1954) Olive Oyl for President (1948) Little Nobody (1936) Kiss Me Cat (1953) Love Me, Love My Mouse (1966) Bewitched Bunny (1954)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: The mistress of a conductor becomes a target for murder after threatening to tell all to her lover's wife.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's partnership - and friendship - is almost torn apart when Caroline drags Max to a seminar to help brand their cupcake business.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline put their apartment up for rent on Airbnb, claiming it offers an authentic "Williamsburg experience," a group of supermodels decide it would be a fun place for a girls' weekend.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get a bank loan so they can mass-produce their cupcake T-shirts to sell at high-end boutiques. Also, the diner gang poses in Chestnut’s barn as part of a holiday decorating contest Sophie entered.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and "Rowdy" Roddy Piper host some incredible in-ring action featuring the likes of Big Boss Man and The Hart Foundation. Bobby "The Brain" Heenan continues to try his hand at hosting his very own show. Plus, updates on SummerSlam, a look at Dusty Rhodes' appearance on The Brother Love Show, and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob chaperones a school trip to the museum and experiences some father-daughter bonding with Louise, while Gene and his gang are on the lookout for exhibits with busts, and not the kind that are sculpted out of stone. Later, on the museum steps, Linda offers her distinctive chanting talents to striking workers.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray purchases a new state-of-the-art vacuum cleaner from an attractive door to door saleswoman and it ignites a housekeeping war between Marie and Debra. Determined to prove to Marie that she's is just as good at "keeping house," Debra attempts to use the new vacuum to find some dirt, any dirt, in Marie's home.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Tempers flare when Marie accuses Debra of not returning a borrowed canister that holds sentimental value. Debra insists that she has already returned the canister, so she is horrified when she discovers that she still has the canister in her possession.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug is on course to break the record for the number of days without an "incident" - no complaints and no broken packages. The older members of the IPS depot aren't too happy that Doug is going to break the record held by their friend.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie get in a fender bender with Deacon and Kelly, and the agreement for Doug and Carrie to pay for the damages to Deacon and Kelly's car causes problems between the two couples. Meanwhile, Spence babysits Deacon and Kelly's son, Kirby, who, not understanding what "allergic" means, believes Spence to be afraid of peanuts and stops calling him the "Spencinator," referring to him as the "Scaredinator" instead.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George finds a new romance--in a women's prison; Kramer buys a chicken, then puts his fowl to foul use; Elaine encourages her shaved-headed beau to grow back his hair.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry's parents believe he is financially unstable; Kramer has trouble sleeping with his girlfriend; Elaine enjoys her position as President of the J. Peterman catalog; George hopes to get his parents' money when they die.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's station wagon is hit by a luxury car that belongs to the rich Mrs. Wellman.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: On April 15th, Dan and Roseanne offer the kids a frantic lesson in family accounting; and in despair finally visit the local IRS office.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Andy does the impossible--he makes everyone in the office miss Dwight, who is forced to take a job at Staples. Meanwhile, Oscar comes back to the office and Michael predictably throws a stereotypical Mexican party to celebrate.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael asks Jim to get a male stripper for Phyllis's office bachelorette party, but he gets a Ben Franklin impersonator instead. Meanwhile, the men's bachelor party goes as planned until Michael becomes uncomfortable with the stripper.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: It was the day of Phyllis and Bob's wedding. Pam was upset because she noticed Phyllis had basically stolen Pam's ideas for her and Roy's would-be wedding. Meanwhile, Michael was very excited to be in charge of pushing Phyllis's father's wheelchair down the aisle.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Ziff Who Came to Dinner
programme.desc: The Simpsons go on a late-night attic search after Bart and Lisa (who are freaked out over a horror movie Homer let them watch) begin hearing voices -- and find Marge's ex-prom date Artie Ziff, who's on the run for cheating the shareholders of his company.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge's bond becomes stronger when they both get drunk on wine, but soon the bond unravels when Homer blames Marge for crashing the car and Marge gets sent to rehab. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa complain to Randall Curtis (a George Lucas-esque science fiction movie director) about his latest film.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart plays a prank on the entire city when he invites everybody for a fake wedding and takes all the gifts so he can return them for store credit. Chief Wiggum catches him and Bart is sent to jail. In jail Bart meets Gina Vendetti who escapes with Bart. Gradually, Gina and Bart get to know and understand each other.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After hearing that Skinner is getting cold feet about their wedding, Edna leaves Skinner at the altar and, after sending back one marriage gift, hooks up with Comic Book Guy. Meanwhile, Homer and Marge have an argument about if their marriage is still solid or not.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a ruined movie night, thanks to Bart and Lisa, Homer and Marge take a vacation away from them, but decide to go to Miami instead of going to see an uncle. When Bart and Lisa catch wind of their parents' secret plan, Bart steals Rod's credit card (just like Homer stole Ned's) and go after them.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al buries his "dead" Dodge in his backyard and has a funeral ceremony for it. The collectors still desire the spare parts and Peg still intends to sell them. At Bud's request, Marcy goes through Al's financial records and declares that Al can't afford a new car. Al decides to prove her wrong and goes to a car dealer. Meanwhile, Jefferson's secret past helps him to locate a fuel pump for Al's old Dodge.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: It's Thanksgiving. In Al's childhood, it was a tradition to eat Aunt Maddie's sweet potato pie on Thanksgiving, so Al takes a trip with Griff to see Aunt Maddie in order to buy some pie. Unfortunately, it turns out that she's just died. Jefferson can't find a turkey for Marcy because they've sold out. Then a stray turkey just happens to follow Kelly home. Will Kelly let the others eat it?

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: The Juggs Have Left the Building
programme.desc: Peggy wants to take a vacation in Branson, Missouri, but Al says he can't afford it. The prospect of all-fried food and only twin beds available, however, make him change his mind. Lucky has to stay behind in the care of the D'Arcys. Then Marcy feels the urge to act out a dirty fantasy at the Bundy's. In Branson, the Bundys get a room at the Deliverance Inn (yes, just like that movie). Peg and Kelly take part in a country music talent contest.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Griff are fired during the holidays and have to take small jobs in the mall. Meanwhile, Kelly and Bud swipe biblical figures from the D'Arcys' nativity scene and Peggy enters the neighborhood Christmas decoration contest.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al is sued by all of the overweight women he has insulted in the past, and they hold a trial in the shoe store. Meanwhile, Kelly and Bud tint the windows on Al's car for his birthday gift.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al is planning a get-together for the guys and they'll be watching wrestling from cable. Al wants Peg to help, but she declines. Then Peg has an accident where she hits her head and gets amnesia. Kelly, Bud, and Al are then amazed to see Peg cooking, cleaning, and doing other housework. They decide to milk the situation for all it's worth.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud confides in Al and says he can't get a single girl to go out with him. Al has a problem too: his boss Gary is being a real bitch. She won't agree to have a break room made for Griff and Al. Bud comes to see Al, but Gary catches him looking at his ass in front of a mirror and expresses her interest in him. Bud gives in to his needs and starts sleeping with Gary. What happens when Al finds out?

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Since Bud is actually earning money, Al starts charging rent for the basement. Bud and Al sign a lease and Bud pays his rent, after which he claims that the "apartment" is in need of repair. A health inspector declares the basement condemned and gives him a month to get the basement repaired. Al then is forced to wear an electronic neck band that prevents him from leaving the basement.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is up in arms when he discovers his neighbor, Julio's family is visiting and may move out to California. However, things really hit the fan when Fred learns that Lamont has asked Julio's sister, Maria out, something Fred is totally against.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred meets a young woman and he quickly announces to Lamont that he has proposed to her. However, Lamont is in for a big surprise when he meets Fred's fiancee who just happens to be Lamont's old flame, whom he would like to marry himself.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Next door neighbor, Julio, has become a very good friend of Lamont's. With Lamont, spending so much free time with Julio, Fred begins to feel left out and decides to try to be more like one of the guys. However, Fred quickly learns the advantages of having so much free time and no one to hoard in on it.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home for the Aged
programme.desc: Lamont breaks the news to Fred that he plans to join the crew of a tramp steamer and sail around the world. A shocked Fred gets another shock, Lamont plans on putting him in a retirement home.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: After finding a dead rat in his carton of milk, Chris decides to become a food inspector.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris must deal with the fact he is ready to undergo ""major"" surgery and he begins by trying to make peace with Sharon.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky Pig doesn't realize that the old hotel where he and Sylvester are spending the night is really part of a ghost town. It's only Sylvester who sees the band of murderous mice trying to do them in, while Porky chalks his fears up to insanity.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: The Tasmanian Devil ends up in Bugs forest.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Disguised as a truant officer, Bugs attempts to rescue Hansel and Gretel from Witch Hazel.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Chasing Tweety all over the rooftops, Sylvester falls off a building and dies. While waiting for his other eight lives, a satanic bulldog goads Sylvester into losing all eight by continuing to chase Tweety into risky situations.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: An inebriated stork must deliver a baby to a giant at the top of a beanstalk, but doesn't feel like flying that high and instead delivers the baby to a normal-sized couple, who try to raise the giant tot as their own.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Ralph is a daydreamer... and he is quick to adapt his current surroundings into new, adventurous dreams.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: After punching in for work, Sam Sheepdog deals with Ralph Wolf's attempts to steal the flock, which this time make use of a balloon, a fake Acme-brand rock and a bicycle-propelled submarine.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A sleep deprived mouse and Hippity Hopper battle Sylvester to keep the beacon light of a lighthouse off.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Riff-Raff (Yosemite) Sam, riding a camel that won't whoa, chases Bugs into a French Foreign-Legion post.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys watch the touching drama ""Joey Buttawipo: My Story;"" Mr. Van Driessen gives them informational books about getting in touch with their feminine sides; they run for Class Treasurer at school.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys head to Maxi-Mart for some Friday night action; they meet a hot biker chick with larceny on her mind.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys accidentally score highly on an IQ test and wind up in the gifted class, where they destroy an actual gifted kid's brain.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis, Can You Spare a Dime?
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head get lessons from a panhandler on how to get cash on the streets.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Raccoons steal Beavis and Butt-Head's nachos, and the boys fight back.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Placed in a ""Scared Straight"" program at school, the boys visit a prison and thrash with some inmates who like Judas Priest and Iron Maiden.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Buzzcut tries to get Beavis and Butt-head to work out.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head learn about evolution and dream of scoring as cavemen.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
An amateur filmmaker documents America's youth culture in a film featuring Beavis and Butt-head.
videos:
The Ramones - "Substitute"
Blur - "Chemical World"
Morphine - "Thursday"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head go tubing on a river and end up lost at sea.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: In this very first Playhouse episode, Pee Wee's friends, including Cowboy Curtis and Miss Yvonne, are introduced when they come over to swim in the pool. Captain Carl would be there too... if he weren't lost! When he calls in a mayday, it takes Conky and Globey to locate him and guide him to the delicious Sandwich Islands. Speaking of delicious, Pee Wee makes Ice cream Soup for the gang when rain drives them back through the Playhouse ""door"".

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: It's no secret that today's secret word is "fun," 'cause Pee Wee just won a dinner for two at a Hawaiian restaurant! The question is -- who's he gonna take? The whole gang, including Captain Carl and Miss Yvonne, "lei"s on the guilt for poor Pee Wee. Even Cowntess the cow has bought a new Moo-Moo! What's Pee Wee to do? Throw his own wild luau, that's what -- and invite the entire Playhouse!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline select a factory to produce their cupcake T-shirts, but become suspicious of the work conditions there due to their overly-enthusiastic employees.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: During a trip to Maine for Meadow's college interviews, Tony sees a man he believes is a relocated mob snitch. Back at home, Carmela receives comfort from Father Phil.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: At Tony's suggestion, Junior is made acting boss after Jackie dies. In therapy, Tony surprises Dr. Melfi with an admission. Carmela and Irina (Tony's mistress) both suffer as Tony's libido takes a nosedive.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor makes a surprising personal discovery. Meanwhile, Michael tasks an indecisive Chidi with coming to an important decision and Jianyu makes a big announcement.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor and Tahani hatch a plan while Michael makes a startling discovery. Meanwhile, Chidi confides in Eleanor after learning some interesting news.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Ten years later. Rhaenyra navigates Alicent's continued speculation about her children, while Daemon and Laena weigh an offer in Pentos.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Tyrion enlists an unlikely ally. Daario entreats Dany to allow him to do what he does best. Jon's warnings fall on deaf ears.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Theon helps Ramsay seize Moat Cailin. The wildlings attack Mole's Town. Sansa comes up with a story to protect Lord Baelish. Daenerys finds out a secret about Jorah Mormont. Oberyn Martell faces Gregor Clegane, the Mountain.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A painter's undiagnosed illness affects his work, and House and his team must look at the man's paintings to determine what's wrong with him.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House tries every delaying tactic available when Wilson forces him to attend his father's funeral. Meanwhile, the team tries to find the cause of a young woman's abdominal pain and hemorrhage that occurred in China.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Flanders' extra ticket to the big game makes him Homer's new best friend, a role that sorely tests Flanders' patience.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Because of Homer losing his driver's license, Marge has to drive everyone everywhere, which is so stressful for her that she accidentally runs over Homer leading to the two seeing a marriage counselor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge revives her interest in art - she once did a painting of Ringo Starr - and Mr. Burns commissions her to do a portrait of him, depicting him as a beautiful man. However, after he has insulted Homer's weight the painting shows him nude, though Marge explains that she intended to show his vulnerability as a frail old man and Burns is highly impressed.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer befriends Mel Gibson just as Mel is completing a remake of Mr. Smith Goes To Washington. Homer convinces Mel to make the film more violent, with disastrous results.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds a friend in his own sister Lisa after Milhouse moves away to Capitol City to live with his divorced mom. Meanwhile Homer goes into panhandling to buy anniversary gifts for Marge. Later on Milhouse returns and Bart goes back being friends with him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Boy Who Knew Too Much
programme.desc: After playing hooky from school, Bart sneaks in to Mayor Quimby's nephew Freddy's birthday party and witnesses a confrontation between Freddy and a waiter, Freddy is accused of beating the waiter but Bart knows what really happened, should he keep quiet or tell the truth and risk getting Cort playing hooky?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons get an abused horse and make him a contender in horse racing. Homer finds out a secret conspiracy about jockeys. Lisa tries to reach President Clinton when the results of a school band talent competition seem tainted.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Stuck at home with a cold, Lisa gets addicted to a video game and neglects her homework. Her A+++ on a test wins the school a grant, but when she confesses that she cheated, Skinner covers it up. Meanwhile, Homer raises a lobster.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Having foiled an attempt by ex-convict Snake to rob the family one night Marge joins the local police force and becomes extremely zealous,dishing out punishments to the family and breaking up Homer's illegal gambling school. When Homer's friend Herman gets him involved in a scam to sell stolen jeans Marge tries to arrest him but gets no help from Wiggum and eventually resigns,complaining of police corruption.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Sideshow Bob is released from prison, runs for mayor as a Republican, and defeats "Diamond" Joe Quimby in the election. But a "Deep-Throat"-type informant tells Bart and Lisa that he was fraudulently elected. It's up to them to find the evidence.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: The Corporation looks for revenge on DX by placing them in some brutal matches including forcing Road Dogg to defend his Hardcore Championship against Mankind. The WWE Universe gets a look at Mr. McMahon's Royal Rumble training as the boss fires Shawn Michaels as Commissioner. Also, Luna targets Sable, The Acolytes kidnap Dennis Knight, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira and David try to earn money by selling cosmetics to the locals, while Johnny heads to Elmdale in hopes of collecting unemployment insurance.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he doesn’t really know. Alexis struggles with her new boyfriend’s love of animals.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline and Max join Sophie at the racetrack as guests of Sophie's new bookie boyfriend, Caroline's reignited love for horse racing gets out of hand.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline discovers that Max didn’t graduate high school by failing to show up for only one exam, she decides to help her best friend get her degree – and reconnect with her estranged mother.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A reality show producer approaches Max and Caroline about shooting an episode of 'Keeping Up With The Kardashians' at their cupcake window.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max is embarrassed when she discovers her Tuesday night boyfriend is a DJ at a grocery chain.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline accidentally loses Han’s expensive fish while cleaning the tank, Max considers selling a sentimental gift she just received from her mother in order to pay for a new one.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline takes an abandoned bicycle in order to start a delivery service for their cupcake business, but discovers that Max never learned how to ride one.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's partnership - and friendship - is almost torn apart when Caroline drags Max to a seminar to help brand their cupcake business.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline put their apartment up for rent on Airbnb, claiming it offers an authentic "Williamsburg experience," a group of supermodels decide it would be a fun place for a girls' weekend.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get a bank loan so they can mass-produce their cupcake T-shirts to sell at high-end boutiques. Also, the diner gang poses in Chestnut’s barn as part of a holiday decorating contest Sophie entered.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline select a factory to produce their cupcake T-shirts, but become suspicious of the work conditions there due to their overly-enthusiastic employees.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Past And The Furious
programme.desc: Caroline is shocked when she receives a Lamborghini for her birthday, which her father ordered for her four years ago. Meanwhile, Oleg has something big planned for Sophie.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max, Caroline, Earl and Han help Oleg move into Sophie’s apartment until she begins turning away all of his things.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are arrested for breaking into a cute guy’s apartment to recover the rings Caroline left behind after she spent the night.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get into a legal battle with two prep school teens who knocked off their cupcake T-shirts and are selling them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline find out that one of their customers at the diner is actually a successful photographer who has been keeping a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Sophie’s wedding shower is interrupted by a hostage situation and Max and Caroline’s cupcake business gets unwanted attention when a tenant is arrested while wearing their company’s T-shirt.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: The Greyhounds try a new strategy that has everyone thinking outside the box. Sam prepares to host a VIP guest at Ola's.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: While watching Henry, Ted fights the urge to spiral when Michelle and Dr. Jacob go on a romantic trip. An online leak has massive implications for Keeley.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Light Sculpture - Paper Plate Illusion - Bacteria Sticker - Flash Fill - Ice Cubes Sticking - Childhood of Chimps - Word Processing - Pouring Along String

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Giant Bubbles - Water Strider Sex I.D. - Banana Surprise - Hero The Robot - Snake Tracker - Uphill Funnel - Muscle Pairs - Wing Bee - Bottle Pickup

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Lester gets a charge out of a static electricity experiment that Beakman conducts to explain thunder and lightning. Surfer Beakman drops by to fix his broken board while explaining how bones mend.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Depicting Liza as the moon and Lester as the Sun, Beakman demonstrates the phases of the moon, answers meaningful questions such as "Do penguins have knees?", and explains how elevators work.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: It's microprocessor madness when Liza creates a video game to uncover the secrets of computers. Beakman dives into dentistry when Lester gets a cavity.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman's timid brother discovers that a visit to the doctor really isn't all that painful. Beakman also traces the evolution of oil from fossils.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Ben Franklin drops by the lab to help Beakman dispell some myths surrounding his inventions. Lester is shocked to learn that his recorded voice is different from the one he hears and Beakman reveals how chemicals react to their new partners.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc:
People once thought that world was flat or nearly flat. It was considered a bit crazy to think of it as a big ball. But it is. You can prove it. One of the big ideas in science is that ideas can be tested. Scientists test claims. If one scientist claims that she or he can fill a balloon with invisible gas using vinegar and baking soda, other scientists can try it and see if they get the same result. Sometimes ideas are wild, extraordinary. And, the claims that go with these way-out ideas are pretty extraordinary as well. The round Earth is an example of an extraordinary claim that needed extraordinary proof. But, there are many people, who believe in extraordinary claims without looking for extraordinary proof. We scientists are always on the lookout.
Sometimes ideas are wild, extraordinary. And, the claims that go with these way-out ideas are pretty extraordinary as well. The round Earth is an example of an extraordinary claim that needed extraordinary proof.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Flowers are an important part of many plants. Plants use flowers to make other plants – to reproduce. Flowers have special parts, called stamens and pistils. When pollen from the stamen finds its way down through the pistil, the flower is pollinated, and seeds start to grow. The seeds eventually find their way to the ground, the seeds sprout, and more plants are born.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin find out that Dad has been dressing as a woman to make friends.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin ends up in detention with Julius, who decides to make Darwin his new target. However, it backfires when Darwin ruins his life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin want to veg out all day, but their plans go awry and they have to save the rest of the family.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Tobias tries to be Gumball’s best friend.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The kids try to help Richard bond with Frankie.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Hot Dog Guy discover why they're so awkward with each other.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E01 - The Cage
programme.desc: The kids convince Mr. Corneille to become a cage fighter and set up a fight against a fearsome Russian fighter.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Alan has lost faith in humanity and turns the world black and white. Gumball must get over his hatred of Alan to bring the color back by singing.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: A group of kids get locked in the school after their parents have a charity gala. They appoint Gumball as leader to get them out but it leads to chaos.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E11 - The Anybody
programme.desc: When Clayton shapeshifts into Gumball to take his detention, he starts framing others for misdemeanors so Gumball and Darwin must track Clayton down to stop him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The discovery of a baby bird fuels Starfire and Cyborg to discover their inner children have been awakened.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin attempts to make the Teen Titans to be taken more seriously by the real heroes from Young Justice.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans visit Starfire's home world of Tamaran to try and learn what makes her so sweet and loving.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven and Beast Boy are surprised to discover that their exes, Terra and Aqualad, are dating each other.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin insists that he is a 'multiple trick pony.'

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Pizza is the source of the Titans' goofy humor.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Two Bumble Bees and a Wasp
programme.desc: When Robin tries to teach the others to have more respect for money, he ends up learning a lesson.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg is looking forward to a marathon of '80s television shows, but the remote appears to be missing.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans immerse themselves into a variety of video game worlds.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven decides to hang out with Rose Wilson because she is cool; the others believe they are lame by comparison so they attend Cool School.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In a deranged tribute to the "I Love Lucy" show, Stimpy lavishes affection on a store-bought oven-ready chicken. Next, Powdered-Toast Man and Waffle-Woman face off in a fight-to-the-death; the world hangs in the balance.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.sub-title: Powdered Toast Man vs. Waffle Woman
programme.desc: A woman tells the story of how her waffle business was ruined because of Powdered Toast. Meanwhile Powdered Toast Man tries to grant a wish for Little Jimmy.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy are about to take that last long walk at the dog pound when a sweet rich old lady falls for them and adopts them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this parody of Pinocchio, Renwaldo, the village of Egg Smithëe, makes a puppet boy name Egg Yölkeo.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren can't stand Stimpy anymore, so he sends Stimpy on a one-way ticket out of town. Then they are hit by a bus and get united (literally) in a cheap hospital.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Needing money to replace the batteries in their TV remote control, Beavis and Butt-head offer to wash a neighbor's vintage car. They take it for a drive to air dry it, and get into an accident with Mr. Anderson.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys reel in various things (and people) while casting from the couch, and then get Stewart in trouble with the law.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After being threatened at school, Beavis and Butt-head wear disguises to class.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head want to go to a concert so they try to kidnap Stewart for ransom money...but quickly get sick of him.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Party Animals vs. Masters of Disaster

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mothers of the World Paddle Rambo will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
To improve the ratings, and to punish the cast for bad acting, Ross sees to it that at least one cast member is slimed between each sketch.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Elvis Presley Is Alive and Working In a Supermarket will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast debates the fantasy and reality of people's beliefs.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Michael Jackson Gets Old and Wrinkled will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross drags Vanessa back onto the show 3 years after she left because she still has an episode under her contract (because the Adoption episode was banned). Meanwhile, Chris is nervous about being fired because of his upcoming birthday and fear that he has reached ""the age"".

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Fat Boys Eat New York will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The show deals with the problems of excess: too much slime, too many cast members, too much wardrobe, etc...

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Pee-Wee Herman Wins the Mr. Macho America Contest will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast is willing to do anything to become cool.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Superman Loses His Tights will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Nick enlists the help of the Genie of the Lost Sock to find Chris' jacket and a camera the Nick borrowed from Ross. In the search, he finds everything that every cast member of the show since it first went on the air had lost.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It is the battle of the still life vs. whole life as the art world takes on the insurance industry. Kenny tries to break into the art world and Vic tries to get Kenny added to his life insurance policy.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Dead End Zone; Hand Job; Wall Buggers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The high societers take on the fishermen in this episode of MXC. Guy, meanwhile, relives lonely days spent on the LeDouche compound on Lake Erie.
Games include: Saddle Sores; Hook, Line, and Swingers; Rotating Surfboard of Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
News anchors challenge lumberjacks (and lumberjills) in this edition of MXC. Also, Vic gives being a lumberjack a try and Guy shows his flair for broadcast journalism.
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Dash to Death; Wall Bangers; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: This installment of MXC features a classic battle between the Mail bags and checkered flags as The Postal Industry takes on Motor Sports. Games - Dead Letter Zone, Foul Balls, Irritable Bowl Syndrome and Log Drop.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The Court Room vs. Rodeo Industry
programme.desc: It's gavel to gavel coverage in MXC land as the classic battle of busting broncos vs. busting criminals takes center stage. Games - Crossing the Pee-O-Grande, Wallbuggers, Little Man in the Boat, and Clear Sphere of Fear.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hot off the success of the College Girls of Spring Break special, MXC holds their second ever all-women show, this time pitting the athletic sports types against the self-made business types. Meanwhile, Vic and Kenny pay tribute to Spike TV's new explosive show, Boom!, by playing with dynamite.
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Rotating Surfboard of Death; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; The Giant G-String of Doom

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: White Collar Careers vs. Blue Collar Careers
programme.desc:
It's Career Day on MXC as white collar workers and their proteges compete against the blue collar workers and their aide de camps.
Games include: Plank Spankers; Slipped Disks of Doom; Mud Butlers; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Comic Book Industry vs. Personal Hygiene
programme.desc:
MXC presents the battle between the people who prevent stinkers against the drawers with pen-and-inkers when the hygiene industry challenges the comic book industry. Also, Vic turns Kenny's unsuccessful Oral-Facializer into the even more unsuccessful Man-Ipulator, and we see the adventures of Megaphone Man when he goes into the lair of the evil doctor Medula Oblongata.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Highballers; Gang Plankers; Runaway Stump

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is forced to work side by side with Kevin at a mall nut stand.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: All the prodigal Lanes come home at once, forcing Jane to temporarily move in with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jake has a heart attack and his mother comes to the Morgendorffer household to take care of him; his heart attack inspires Quinn to study to become a heart doctor; two radio DJs broadcast their show outside Lawndale High.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn have to take a road trip to bail Jane and Trent out of a traffic violation.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Secret service agents visit Lawndale High, and urge students to keep an eye of for students that might be different, and thus a threaten their safety, as well as the safety of the country. Though Daria & Jane become suspect numbers one and two, they actually start to notice an outbreak of weirdness among their fellow students, faculty members, and even relatives.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The whole school goes on a mandatory-voluntary harbor cruise to raise money via casino gambling.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jane starts dating a guy, to the annoyance of her misanthropic friend Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: For an economics project, Daria and Jodie team up to start a company.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A mechanical revolution has begun as all house hold objects unite to rebel against their makers. Luckily DM's car is equipped with an anti-mutiny module, so he and Penfold can go after Baron Greenback. However, the terrible toad is busy hiding from his Frog Head Flyer. The real instigator behind this uprising turns out to be an enormous sentient computer calling itself Mega-Brain.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dangermouse and Penfold are ordered to find the cause of a dreadful noise terrorizing the world. It seems to be coming from the North Pole, where they find a giant hole housing a secret workshop that keeps the Earth rotating. One of the workers there, Stanley Atkington explains how his colleague Obidiah's attempting to take over now that they're boss 'The Gaffer's away on holiday.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Danger Mouse on the Orient Express
programme.desc: While on holiday in Venice, DM and Penfold stumble upon a fiendish plot of Greenback's to raise buildings all over Europe and lure all tourists to his museum of Barry Mannilow album covers. Colonel K's office has arranged transport for them aboard the Orient Express, but Greenback has also charted the train for the Euro Master Crooks convention in Paris.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback seeks a truce with Dangermouse to save the Earth from being attacked by an alien army. They agree to meet on Greenback's space station, but Dangermouse and Penfold are unaware they are flying into a trap.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr. Frankenstoat has constructed a fiendish device to make electric Vampoids infused with Count Duckula's personality. Danger Mouse infiltrates his castle intend on destroying the machine, but Penfold pushes the start button, releasing a flock of bats.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Where There's a Well There's a Way
programme.desc: Danger Mouse is sent to find Merlin's magic ink well which will grant the finder a wish.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Top secret blueprints for a world shattering device have been stolen from Puttinghamdown Research station by Mac the Fork. He teams up with Dudley Poyson to build it as Dangermouse and Penfold race to Glasgow to stop them.
